Q8 Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

In the following question, a part of the sentence is given in bold. Choose the correct alternative that best improves the bold part. If no improvement is needed, select “No improvement.”
The Vice Chancellor congratulated the blind student for his success in the examination.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
The verb “congratulate” is typically followed by the preposition “on” when referring to the reason for the congratulation.
Correct sentence: “The Vice Chancellor congratulated the blind student on his success in the examination.”

Q9 Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Read the passage carefully and choose the best answer to the following question from the four alternatives given below.
In the past 50 years, doctors across the world have accepted the practice to prescribe antibiotics at the first sign of a trivial infection or treat patients with a handful of antibiotics. These days it is not uncommon to see practitioners prescribing multiple antibiotics without any real indication or relevance for such a combination of drugs. Antibiotics have traditionally been known as miracle drugs, but there is growing evidence that they are overworked miracles, especially in countries like ours where there is easy access to drugs across the counter, including antibiotics. We cannot think of a return to pre-antibiotic days. Yet the unbridled use of these agents is inexorably propelling us in that direction.
Antibiotics are called overworked miracles because:

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
The passage clearly points out that antibiotics are being excessively used, so they are called overworked miracles.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

 
 
Q17 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

During a temperature inversion in the atmosphere, the movement of air pollutants is restricted.
Under such conditions, how do the pollutants generally behave?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Under normal conditions, warm air rises, enabling vertical mixing and pollutants dispersion, but
inversion reverses this dynamics thereby leading to accumulation and stagnation of pollution
close to ground below the inversion layer.

Q29 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following errors in chaining is/are non-compensating error(s)?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, b, c, d)
Compensating errors in chaining are those which are equally likely to be positive or negative and
thus tend to cancel each other out when measurements are repeated over long distances.
Non-compensating errors are one-sided; they always occur in the same direction.

Q36 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

When the COD/BOD ratio of a wastewater sample is found to be very high, it usually indicates which of the following characteristics of the organic matter present?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
A high COD/BOD ratio means that much of the oxygen demand is due to substances not easily
broken down biologically i.e. low biodegradability.

Q54 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

In the design and operation of trickling filters, which of the following statement is incorrect?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
The term filter is misleading in trickling filters, since few of the physical processes associated
with filtration through granular media function in trickling filters. Instead, sorption and subsequent
biological oxidation are the primary means of food removal.
